subject: Discrete Random Variables
topic: §16 Expected Value of a Function of a Discrete Random Variable
question: Consider a random variable $X$ whose probability mass function is given by $p(x) = \begin{cases} 0.1 & x = -3 \\ 0.2 & x = 0 \\ 0.3 & x = 2.2 \\ 0.1 & x = 3 \\ 0.3 & x = 4 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ Let $F(x)$ be the corresponding cdf. Find $E(F(X))$.
